reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All gates passed. Code reading is factually correct: line 154 of subscriber_beacon_blocks.go passes sidecar.Index directly to BroadcastDataColumnSidecar instead of calling peerdas.ComputeSubnetForDataColumnSidecar(sidecar.Index) as done correctly in proposer.go:488. This violates the PeerDAS specification. However, on current mainnet configuration (DataColumnSidecarSubnetCount = NumberOfColumns = 128), the modulo operation columnIndex % 128 == columnIndex for all valid columns [0-127], making the bug functionally a no-op. The bug would only manifest if parameters diverge (e.g., 256 columns with 128 subnets). Downgraded from Medium to Informational due to zero current exploitability on deployed networks. 01e reference: outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json line 914-929, invariant 'Publishing data columns must only occur on subnets the node is subscribed to (custody/sampling)'.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: forall col: published(col, subnet) => subscribed(subnet, custody) || subscribed(subnet, sampling)
